# Requirements Quality Checklist: Client & Watchlist Management

**Purpose**: Pre-plan gate — validate that the spec's requirements are complete, clear, consistent, measurable, and well-bounded before `/speckit-plan`. These items test the *requirements writing*, not the eventual implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-06
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit-checklist` (focus: overall requirements QA; depth: standard pre-plan gate; audience: author/reviewer).

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are requirements defined for the full client lifecycle — create, retrieve, update, suspend/reactivate? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, §US1]
- [ ] CHK002 Are requirements defined for the full watchlist lifecycle — create, list, retrieve, rename, deactivate? [Completeness, Spec §FR-017, §US2]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the reconciliation of existing `users.client_id` values into real client rows specified completely (no orphaned users, audit FK preserved)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK004 Are audit-logging requirements stated for every create/update/suspend/deactivate on clients and watchlist configuration? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK005 Are the documented default values specified for every optional configuration (cadence, severity threshold)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK006 Are requirements present for who may create a brand-new client (operator) vs. configure it (admin)? [Completeness, Spec §US1, §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK007 Is the new-schema/migration obligation stated without leaking implementation detail beyond conventions? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018]

## Requirement Clarity & Measurability

- [x] CHK008 Is the canonical, ordered set of named severity levels explicitly enumerated (not just exemplified with "e.g.")? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007] — Resolved: exactly `non-serious` < `serious` < `life-threatening`, default `serious`.
- [x] CHK009 Is the supported set of cadence values explicitly enumerated, and is the default cadence named? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-006, §Assumptions] — Resolved: `{daily, weekly, monthly}`, default `weekly`.
- [x] CHK010 Is the budget warning threshold stated as a fixed value or as an admin-configurable one (the spec says "e.g., 80%")? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010] — Resolved: platform-fixed at 80% for v1.
- [x] CHK011 Is "monthly period" defined precisely enough to be testable (calendar month vs. rolling 30 days; timezone/boundary)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, §Assumptions] — Resolved: calendar month, UTC.
- [x] CHK012 Is the resolution of the empty-watchlist case decided (reject-on-activation OR treat-as-nothing-to-monitor), rather than left as an either/or? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-016] — Resolved: reject; ≥1 item required.
- [ ] CHK013 Are the success criteria measurable and free of implementation detail (e.g., "zero cross-tenant access", "exactly one audit entry")? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003, §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK014 Is "in-flight/critical work allowed to finish" defined clearly enough to be verifiable, given the atomic "run" is deferred to spec 11? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011, §Assumptions]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK015 Is configuration ownership stated consistently as per-watchlist (cadence/severity/budget) across user stories, FRs, and key entities? [Consistency, Spec §US3–§US5, §FR-006–§FR-012]
- [ ] CHK016 Are the two distinct keyword concepts — watchlist *retrieval* keywords vs. deferred *severity* custom keywords — kept terminologically distinct throughout? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, §FR-008, §Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK017 Do budget-related statements consistently scope spend, warning, and soft-cap to a single watchlist (no leftover "client" wording)? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010–§FR-012, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK018 Are uniqueness rules consistent — client name unique platform-wide, watchlist name unique per client — wherever each is referenced? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK019 Are the role-authorization rules (admin writes; reviewer views own client; unauthenticated refused first) consistent across FR-013 and the user stories? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, §US2]

## Acceptance Criteria & Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Does every functional requirement have at least one corresponding acceptance scenario or success criterion? [Traceability, Spec §FR-001–§FR-018, §SC]
- [ ] CHK021 Are alternate-path requirements covered (creating a second watchlist; different cadences per watchlist; sibling watchlist unaffected by a peer's cap)? [Coverage, Spec §US2, §US3, §US5]
- [ ] CHK022 Are exception-path requirements covered (duplicate name, out-of-range/malformed config, duplicate item, removing absent item)? [Coverage, Spec §US2, §US3, §US4, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK023 Are recovery/state-restoration requirements covered (budget auto-resume at month reset; admin raises budget; watchlist reactivation)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-012, §US5, §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK024 Are deactivated-state requirements covered for both client and watchlist (excluded from monitoring, data preserved)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-002, §FR-017, §Edge Cases]

## Non-Functional & Compliance Requirements

- [ ] CHK025 Are multi-tenant isolation requirements stated as enforceable and measurable (refuse read/modify/enumerate of another client's data — the NON-NEGOTIABLE principle)? [Coverage, Spec §FR-004, §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK026 Is the elevated coverage obligation for config write paths stated with a concrete threshold? [Measurability, Spec §SC-009]
- [ ] CHK027 Are secrets/config-discipline requirements (Vault for secrets, no client config in env files) captured? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK028 Is data preservation on deactivation (no destructive delete) stated consistently as a compliance/traceability requirement for clients and watchlists? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, §FR-017]

## Dependencies, Assumptions & Bounded Scope

- [ ] CHK029 Are out-of-scope boundaries explicit and unambiguous (React Admin Console deferred; MeSH validation → spec 4; custom severity keywords → spec 8; spend metering generated elsewhere)? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions, §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK030 Are cross-spec dependencies on reused spec-1/spec-2 foundations (client_id boundary, audit human-actor FK, role guards) documented as assumptions rather than re-specified? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]

## Notes

- Check items off as the spec is confirmed/updated: `[x]`. An unchecked item flags a requirement-writing gap to resolve (or consciously defer) before planning.
- 2026-06-06: CHK008–CHK012 resolved — severity levels/default, cadence set/default, fixed 80% warning threshold, UTC calendar-month period, and empty-watchlist rejection are now concretely specified in the spec.
